definition.name: PSD_cone
definition.definition: The positive semidefinite (PSD) cone of order n, denoted S^n_+ (real case) or H^n_+ (complex/Hermitian case), is the set of n×n positive semidefinite matrices: S^n_+ = {X ∈ S^n : X ≽ 0} where S^n is the space of real symmetric n×n matrices, or H^n_+ = {X ∈ H^n : X ≽ 0} where H^n is the space of Hermitian n×n matrices. Key dimensional facts: dim_R(S^n) = n(n+1)/2 and dim_R(H^n) = n². A matrix X ∈ S^n_+ of rank r generates a face of dimension r(r+1)/2 in S^n_+; a matrix X ∈ H^n_+ of rank r generates a face of dimension r² in H^n_+. The classical Barvinok-Pataki bound for real SDP is r(r+1)/2 ≤ m; for complex SDP it is r² ≤ m. The state space S(M_n(C)) of the matrix algebra is the trace-1 slice of H^n_+, so faces in S(M_n(C)) have dimension r² - 1 (one less due to trace normalisation). References: Barvinok, Discrete Comput. Geom. 25, 2001; Pataki, Math. Oper. Res. 23(2), 1998.
